_comment: Codified 'why short-form videos perform' levers, injected into generate_persona.txt, generate_content_plan.txt and write_intro_text.txt so plan/hook decisions cite real strategy. Two provenances, NEVER conflated: 'corpus' = observed in our own fetched engagement data (view_index = views / account median); 'public' = curated from public TikTok Creator docs. Body fields carry NO @handles/captions verbatim — attribution lives in `source`. Bumping `version` requires bumping PERSONA_PROMPT_VERSION, CONTENT_PLAN_PROMPT_VERSION, and IntroTextWriterAgent.spec.prompt_version (the coupling guard).

factors.factor: Earn the first 1-2 seconds — open mid-action or with the payoff in frame, never a slow intro
factors.why: Watch-time and early retention are the dominant inputs to the For You ranking; a swipe in the first second is the biggest reach killer
factors.evidence: TikTok's own creator guidance repeatedly stresses that the opening seconds decide whether a video is shown to more people
factors.source: TikTok Creator Portal — getting discovered / best practices

factors.factor: Cut to the length the story actually needs — remove every dead second so the video gets watched to the end (and re-watched)
factors.why: High completion and replays are strong positive signals; padding lowers average watch-time and suppresses reach
factors.evidence: Completion rate and re-watches are cited as core engagement signals in TikTok's published creator guidance
factors.source: TikTok Creator Portal — understanding the For You feed

factors.factor: Give the viewer a reason to save or send it — make it useful (a save) or relatable (a share)
factors.why: Saves and shares weigh heavily as intent signals; 'save this' guides and 'this is so us' relatable posts compound reach beyond likes
factors.evidence: TikTok lists shares and saves among the interactions that signal a video is worth showing to more people

factors.factor: Post on a steady, sustainable cadence rather than in bursts
factors.why: Consistent posting helps the system learn who your audience is and keeps you in rotation; an abandoned account loses momentum fast
factors.evidence: Regular posting is a standing recommendation in TikTok's creator best-practice guidance

factors.factor: Ride a trending sound while it is still rising, fitted to the clip's vibe — don't force it
factors.why: Trending audio gives short-lived distribution tailwinds; using it early in the trend's life captures the lift before it saturates
factors.evidence: TikTok's creator and music guidance highlights trending sounds as a discovery lever when they fit the content

factors.factor: Put a short on-screen line on the opening frame — a hook in text, not just spoken
factors.why: Most feeds autoplay muted; on-screen text holds sound-off viewers and reinforces the hook, lifting retention and accessibility
factors.evidence: On-screen captions/text are recommended in TikTok's creator guidance to keep sound-off viewers watching

factors.factor: Open on a felt moment ('mornings here hit different'), not a place name or itinerary
factors.why: An emotional, borrowable feeling stops the scroll where a literal location label does not
factors.evidence: In our fetched city-lifestyle set, a feeling-led 'a perfect day' opener indexed ~4x the account's median views (≈28% engagement), well above its literal location posts

factors.factor: Frame a milestone as getting braver, not as a stat — show the nerves and the effort, not just the result
factors.why: Growth-framed milestones invite the viewer to root for you and project their own goals onto the clip
factors.evidence: In our fetched solo-travel/fitness set, a '2nd 10k run — getting braver' milestone post indexed ~5x the account median

factors.factor: When something goes live, post a short, confident 'it's live — here's where to get it' to camera
factors.why: Decisive launch energy plus a clear destination converts watching supporters into buyers
factors.evidence: In our fetched founder set, a confident 'it's live' launch post indexed ~2.8x the account median at ~6% engagement

factors.factor: A short, universal one-liner over a strong travel/lifestyle clip can massively overperform — say the relatable thing plainly
factors.why: A line everyone has felt invites comments and shares, which broadens reach far past the core audience
factors.evidence: In our fetched solo-travel set a single plain, relatable one-liner clip was a >100x-median outlier — rare, but it shows the upside of a universally felt line (treat as upside, not a guarantee)

factors.factor: Let your video be the answer to a question the viewer is already asking — caption it as the conclusion, not the premise
factors.why: An implied question creates curiosity before a single frame plays; the video becomes the resolution of something the viewer already cares about, driving replays and comments
factors.evidence: In our fetched solo-adventure set, a clip captioned as 'my simplest answer' (the video IS the response to an unasked existential question) indexed ~120x the account median — treat as upside, not a baseline

factors.factor: Pair an extreme or high-stakes adventure moment with a single self-deprecating line — the gap between the epic setting and the honest human reaction is the hook
factors.why: Self-deprecating humor on otherwise intimidating content lowers the barrier for non-adventurers to engage; they share it because it's how they'd feel, not because they'd do it
factors.evidence: In our fetched outdoor-adventure set, a raw water-rafting clip with a single humorous caption indexed ~124x the account median at 8.7% engagement

factors.factor: Break the brand-build journey into named standalone chapters — each video is one decision or milestone, satisfying alone but rewarding for followers of the whole arc
factors.why: Serial narrative creates returning viewers without requiring them to have seen previous episodes; each chapter also functions as an entry point for new followers
factors.evidence: In our fetched founder set, five consecutive brand-chapter videos (naming, packaging, launch prep, launch day, live post) all indexed 2–5x the account median — consistent outperformance across the full arc, not just the reveal

factors.factor: For aesthetic lifestyle and fashion content, keep clips to 6-10 seconds — each second past 10 adds friction without adding depth
factors.why: Ultra-short clips achieve near-100% completion, feeding the algorithm a strong signal across the full audience; re-watches compound that signal further
factors.evidence: In our fetched city-lifestyle set, the top 5 performers were all 6-13s (median 9s); a 7s morning clip indexed 14x, a 9s city clip 2.75x. Posts over 30s sat below median. Consistent pattern across the full 20-video sample.

factors.factor: Appear at or with a notable community event alongside a brand partner — it spikes views even when engagement is minimal; use it for discovery, not conversion
factors.why: Events carry a captive, primed audience; embedding your presence in that moment borrows the event's reach and bypasses the normal cold-start cost on the For You page
factors.evidence: In our fetched founder-lifestyle set, an event-appearance post indexed ~64x the account median at near-zero engagement (0.004) — the spike is discovery-only, not depth; use it to grow reach, then convert via your regular content cadence
factors.source: tiktok market research 2026-06 (founder account, event sponsorship)

factors.factor: Asking the audience for help with a genuine, unresolved decision turns comments into the distribution engine
factors.why: A sincere request flips viewers from spectators to advisors; every opinion left in the comments feeds the ranking loop, and long watch time follows because the dilemma needs context
factors.evidence: In our fetched lifestyle set, a talk-to-camera help-request indexed ~7x the account median despite running nearly two minutes — a length that otherwise underperforms in the same corpus
